extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won stage 1 by jumping early when sprinters hesitated in disorganized finale, holding off EWAN Caleb after multiple failed moves.
tactical_patterns: KEIZER Martijn took intermediate sprint with PATERSKI Maciej, bonus seconds critical for team car positioning on stage 2.
BMC's OEHLINGER Daniel strung out pace at 2km with no sprinter, disrupting leadout trains fatally for organized teams.
Quick-Step attacked without sprinter present creating chaos; teams brought wrong rosters expecting controlled finish.
SAGAN launched sprint when leadouts collapsed, recognizing hesitation beats organization when finale becomes disorganized.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: spotted timing window when sprinter teams hesitated, launched early and held power to line despite disorganized approach
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: second place but MEZGEC Luka leadout exhausted early, forced to improvise positioning behind rival trains
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: negative: died slow death as leadout man, looking for help 900m out indicating insufficient preparation
rider_signals.HOELGAARD Daniel: neutral: BMC used him to disrupt at 2km without sprinter, tactical move but caught before finale
rider_signals.BONIFAZIO Niccolò: neutral: top five finish suggests opportunism works when crash and chaos eliminate organized trains
rider_signals.FARRAR Tyler: neutral: third place result shows veterans capitalize when leadouts fail and younger sprinters crash
